1.content:
I will never forget my 5th birthday party.

All my friends came to celebrate with me. The party had a puppy theme, and we all wore homemade dog costumes. We all had a great time. Even my big sister, Karen, who was three years older than I was. Even my little brother, Jeff, who was three years old, and deaf since he was born. The moms kept an eye on us, and Dad was inside, watching TV until it was time to start up the grill.

But what really made the day memorable was when the spaceship flew over the house.

I'm not kidding, the spaceship flew right over my house. We all looked up at it. Dad came out, to see what was going on. Even Jeff looked. He couldn't hear it, but he told us later that he could feel the vibrations in the air.

When the spaceship went by, some of the moms went to see where it was going. Mom and Dad pulled us kids inside, though, and we all watched it on TV. As we watched, we knew that our world and our lives would never be the same again.

In my case, truer words were never spoken.

******************************


The ship landed on an empty field, where they were supposed to start building some big box store. The local news got there, with a reporter who kept asking us who was inside it, and what they wanted, as if we knew any more than he did.

Then, a hatch opened up, and they put what looked like a glass box right down next to the reporter. There was a flash, and, suddenly, somebody was inside the box, and he stepped outside.

It was our first look at a real live alien. He was the same size as a human, with the same basic shape, but the similarities ended there. His skin was gold, with spots of darker gold on his head, where humans have hair. His eyes were iridescent, with vertically slit pupils. He had six fingers on each hand.

He extended a hand to the reporter, who handed him the microphone he was using.

"We bring greetings to you," he said. "We are Metaturians. A disaster has compelled us to leave our homeworld and travel the starways. Yours is the first planet we have found which is capable of supporting life. With your permission, we will live here, by your side."

After that, what could we say?

******************************


This wasn't the only Metaturian ship to come to Earth. There was at least one on every inhabited continent. Our various transmissions helped them learn to speak the native tongues in the places where they'd settled.

Before long. Mom was entertaining Metaturian neighbors in the kitchen, and I had brand-new friends.

As I said, the arrival of the Metaturians changed our lives. Mine, most of all.

If they had gone in a different direction, or found life elsewhere, then I would have grown up an ordinary human girl named Rennie Weaver.

Instead, I became a Metaturian girl named Aayala.

This is my story.
1.title: How It Began

11.content:
It was almost five years after the Arrival, so you know that I was almost 10. I was playing with some of my best friends, Corran Edozoik, Galen Vulrn, and Iriel Straengi. The V'zod, the ship that the Metaturians came to our community in, had sat, disused, in the field where they had landed. Arrival Day (and, therefore, my birthday) was about a week away, and preparations for the celebration were underway.

I don't remember who, but somebody had the idea to have some fun with the teleporter their representative had used to disembark that day. It might have been Corran's idea, since he was the first to go into the booth. He went in, there was a flash, and Corran wasn't in the booth anymore, he was onboard the ship. He called after us, so we decided that it must be okay. Galen was next, followed by Iriel, and I brought up the rear.

Teleportation wasn't any easier to describe going through it than it was watching it happen: I was inside the booth, there was a flash, and I was inside a different booth, inside the ship.

That was when the police came in, two human officers. "Come out of here, you kids," one officer said. "This stuff is nothing to play with."

"Yeah," the other officer added, "you could hit the wrong button, and who knows what could happen?"

We grumbled a bit, about grownups always having to spoil our fun, but we went with them off the ship.

Little did we know that, in my case, the officers' warning had come too late.
11.title: Fateful Play

111.content:
It was at the Arrival Day celebration when we got our first clue what had happened.

I was having fun with all my friends at the festivities, even with Sarah Douglas, who had had to sneak in; her mother didn't seem to like the Metaturians for some reason. It was a hot day, so we were all drinking big cups of soda. We were sitting down. And I was bothered by this persistent itch, right in the middle of my chest.

As I started my second cup of soda, I started having trouble focusing my eyes. It was like, whatever I was looking at, there were suddenly two of them. I shook my head, hoping to clear my vision, but no such luck.

"Are you okay, Rennie?" asked Missi Pyle, another of my friends.

"Ever'thing looksh funny," I replied. Now, I was having trouble talking, too.

"C'mon," said Susan Oliver, another friend, "let's go find your folks."

We got up to go, and my legs suddenly didn't want to do what I wanted. If we weren't already going to look for my parents, I would have said we should go get them. With Missi on one side, and Susan on the other, they half-carried me along.

It wasn't hard to find my parents, since Mom was about a month away from having her fourth baby. "What's going on, girls?" Dad asked.

"Rennie's not feeling well, Mr. Weaver," Missi replied.

"It seemed to come on all of a sudden," Susan added.

Since I couldn't walk or talk the way I could when we first got there, my folks agreed that something was wrong with me, and they took me to the hospital, with my friends in tow.

******************************


At the hospital, they ran some tests. Then, they ran some other tests, since the first tests didn't seem to help. The whole family was taking turns hugging me. What could possibly be the matter with me?

Finally, the doctor came back, along with a Metaturian doctor. "This is Dr. Olkaela," the human doctor said.

Dr. Olkaela came right to me. "Did you do anything recently, Rennie, that you don't normally do?"

"We - we played on the ship," I said. "Lasht week."

"What did you do there?"

"We wen' through the tel'porter."

Dr. Olkaela nodded. "That must be the cause," she said.

"What are you talking about?" Mom asked. "What's wrong with my daughter?"

"To begin with her present condition, Mrs. Weaver," Dr. Olkaela said, "Rennie is intoxicated."

"But all we had was some soda," Missi said.

"But you girls didn't go through the teleporter, did you? Only Rennie and her Metaturian friends."

"What do you mean?"

"I'm afraid the teleporter was never programmed to recognize human DNA. When Rennie went through, it didn't understand why she was different, so it... 'corrected' the problem."

"What are you saying?"

"The teleporter made Rennie genetically Metaturian. And, now, her body is changing to reflect this."
111.title: Strange Symptoms

1111.content:
"How could this have happened?" Worried, Mom demanded. "Wasn't the ship fenced off, with warning signs posted?"

"Children, human and Metaturian alike, often ignore phrases such as 'off limits' and 'keep out,' when there is a promise of fun to be had." Ruefully, Dr. Olkaela explained. "What we need to focus on now is not blame, but damage control."

"Will Rennie be okay, Doctor?" Concerned, Dad demanded.

Dr. Olkaela sighed. "Your daughter will be in for a... frustrating transition." Calmly, she replied. "Much like a human's transitioning from male to female, Miss Rennie will be experiencing a new type of puberty; one which will be challenging on a mental, physical, and social level." Pausing, she added, "Your daughter will survive this, but I fear she is no longer human."

Mom began to sob, hugging me, while Dad merely sighed, shaking his head. "What can we do from here?" Serious, he asked.

"I suggest we start by discussing the 'dos and do nots' of Metaturian health and development." Calmly, Dr. Olkaela replied. "From there, I can assign a Metaturian social worker, to help you guide your daughter through her transition."

"I have a few Metaturian friends I can call upon for advice." Shaken, Mom said. "Tell me,... has this happened before?"

Dr. Olkaela nodded. "There have been rumors of it happening on other continents, but I hesitate to speak of them." Wincing, she replied. "They were not as fortunate."

Mom turned pale as Dad turned to me with a scolding expression. "You were lucky, young lady." Serious, he said.

That was when I began to cry.

Dad's face immediately softened, and he joined Mom in comforting me. "We will figure this out." Softly, he said. "Nothing will change the fact you are our little princess."
1111.title: What Now?

11111.content:
"As Rennie discovered for herself," Dr. Olkaela said, "caffeine is an intoxicant to Metaturians. So don't let her drink any more, at least until she is an adult."

The way I was feeling, I was in no hurry to repeat this experience, but Mom and Dad promised to keep an eye on what I drank in the future. "How about alcohol?"

"It's harmless to us. She'll be able to handle liquor as if it were water."

"Should we watch out for anything else?"

"Your inexpensive milk chocolate is a mood elevator for us. If she's in a low mood, and there's a happy occasion about to happen, a chocolate bar will have her celebrating. But don't let her have any before sad movies or funerals, or people will wonder why she's sitting there with a smile on her face."

"Duly noted."

"As for premium dark chocolate, well,... that's not something for one your daughter's age, so keep it away from her, as well."

"Thank you, Doctor. Can Rennie come home now?"

"Well, she could be violently ill at any time, and she'll wake up tomorrow with grellan-gex, or 'hangover,' as you call it. But I suppose that she can recover at home. Unless you'd rather be here for it... ?"

I shook my head. "I do' wanna shpen' my birfday inna hoshpital."

"Oh. Well, happy birthday, Rennie. I'm sorry you won't feel like celebrating."

******************************


I threw up once while I was getting ready, again on the way home, and once more at home. With my stomach so upset, Mom and Dad didn't dare give me any chocolate, even though it might have helped my mood. My friends, human and Metaturian, decided to put off my party until tomorrow. Mom also said that Mrs. Edozoik would come tomorrow, too, so I could recover from being drunk.

Fine by me. Being drunk didn't bother me half as much as the fact that I was no longer human.
11111.title: Returning home.

111111.content:
The following morning, I woke up with my head pounding and my mouth and throat feeling parched. I opened my eyes, only to quickly shut them, as the light of the room stung my eyes. From what little I saw, however, I was not certain I wanted them open just yet. Was the room spinning just now? I felt weak and ached all over, making me want to vomit again, which I did, though I didn't know where it ended up.

"This is horrible." Softly, I moaned. "How can anyone keep getting drunk, feeling like this in the morning?"

"Good question." Came a feminine voice.

I moaned and covered my ears, as the response almost felt like shouting. "Not so loud." Grumbling, I complained.

"Sorry, kiddo." More softly, the woman replied. "The first grellan-gex is always the worst to endure."

Slowly, uncomfortably, i was able to open my eyes enough to see that the woman in my room was Mrs. Edozoik, a Metaturian friend of the family. "Do you have anything for this headache?" Wincing, I asked.

Mrs. Edozoik smiled patiently before giving me a tall glass of a strange colored liquid. It almost looked like a liquefied pixie stick. "It is called gorth-tarak." Smiling ruefully, she explained. "It's basically a detox smoothie... just don't ask what's in it."

Sighing, I took the glass from Mrs. Edozoik and quickly drank it down, fearing it would taste terrible. I was not disappointed. "Disgusting!" Coughing, I complained.

"It's designed to detoxify your body, not be good to the taste." Smiling, Mrs. Edozoik said. "Your symptoms should clear up within a half hour. Until then, I suggest you rest up for your belated birthday party."

I sighed, still feeling weak. "Are Mom and Dad mad at me?" Worried, I asked.

"That's the grellan-gex talking." Patiently, Mrs. Edozoik said. "Still, no one is mad at you, they're just worried. But do not fear, we will help you through this."

With that, she quietly left the room for me to rest.
111111.title: The Next Morning

1111111.content:
About half an hour later, I was feeling better, so I sat up. It looked like I'd thrown up on the bed. Mrs. Edozoik had cleaned me up as much as she could, but I still needed to wash my face and hair.

In my bathroom, I took off my pajamas, and saw why my chest was itching yesterday: I now had three nipples. This brought home what was happening to me. By the time Mom brought Baby No. 4 into the world, I wouldn't look like the rest of the family: I would be a Metaturian. I checked myself for a tail, but I didn't have one. Not yet, anyway.

******************************


After being drunk and sick all day yesterday, my appetite had come back. Mom and Dad made sure I had plenty of caffeine-free things to drink, and Dad even let me have some beer. It didn't taste like anything I'd had before, and the alcohol didn't affect me at all.

At noon, all my friends came, and my belated 10th birthday party was ready to begin.

Mrs. Edozoik and her son, Corran, started by taking a glass out of the cupboard and pouring something into it. "You've dealt with one of the worst things about being a Metaturian," Mrs. Edozoik said, "so here's one of the best things: It's called nekta juice."

I was skeptical, as it looked muddy, but it tasted wonderful. It was like mango-flavored beer.

Most of the gifts from my friends would be things I could keep using (books, movies, games), although Missi apologized for getting me combs and barrettes for my hair. "It's okay," I told her, "you couldn't have known I was going to change. I'll use these every day, for as long as I can."

"You'll need to wait for your gifts from us," Mom said. "You'll need shoes for six-toed feet, and pants and underthings that can accommodate a tail."

"I understand, Mom."

Jeff signed, *What will having six fingers do for your sign language?*

I laughed and signed, *Think of it like speaking with an accent.*

That said, I was glad that I'd be done changing by the time school started.
1111111.title: Birthday party take 2.

11111111.content:
After the party, Mrs. Edozoik took my hand. "Let's go back to your room, Rennie," she said. "There are a few things I need to tell you, privately."

I went with her, wondering what she meant. Dr. Olkaela had already told me I was becoming a Metaturian. What else was there?

Once we were in my room, Mrs. Edozoik began taking off her clothes. "Ma'am? Should you be doing this?"

"It's how we Metaturians do it," she replied, down to her underwear. "My mother taught me this way. Her mother taught her. And so on." She was now completely naked.

"And you'll teach your daughter, if you have one?" I asked, looking at her three breasts.

She shook her head. "I had a difficult time getting pregnant, and Corran's birth nearly killed me. My doctors told me I shouldn't try again. So you're as close as I'm going to come to teaching a daughter."

"I'm sorry to hear that, Mrs. Edozoik."

"Samara, please."

"Samara. Is having a baby usually so hard?"

"I was a special case. No, you'll probably have a baby just fine when you're ready."

"What else is it like?"

"Well, pregnancy lasts for 48 weeks, so about 11 months. Labor is usually 24 hours, give or take. Of course, before you have to deal with that, you'll have vrokaela."

"Vrokaela?"

"Menstruation. It should be about three years or so away for you. For most women, it's not difficult, but I have some problems. It may be connected to the problems I had with having Corran."

"Again, I'm sorry you had so much trouble, Samara. But I'm glad you're here to help me, now."

She smiled. "And I'm glad to help you, little one." She got dressed and left.
11111111.title: The Facts of Metaturian Life

111111111.content:
As the day progressed, my headache returned with a passion, and it was beginning to make me moody. Mom noticed this immediately, and called Samara on speakerphone so I could hear.

After Mom explained what was happening, Samara sighed. "I forgot to mention that." Ruefully, she said. "It's called Te-fang, a part of Metaturian puberty, where our teeth go through a transformation."

"My... teeth?" Wincing, I asked.

"Metaturian teeth are considerably harder and sharper than humans', due to the tough skin of our fruits and vegetables on our world." Calmly Samara explained. "Only they do not harden or sharpen until puberty starts."

"So... basically, she is teething again?" Trying to understand, Mom said.

"Exactly." Calmly, Samara said. "The pain of her teeth is dull, but enough to cause headaches and mood swings." She giggled slightly. "Just wait until you get The Talk for Metaturian women."

I sighed, rolling my eyes. "I can't wait... " Sarcastically, I said.

Mom held back a giggle. "You were going to get The Talk one way or another, young lady." Bemused, she said.

"I'll pick up some teething products and drop them off later today." Kindly, Samara said. "In the meantime, I suggest Rennie rests with a cold cloth."

With that, she and Mom ended the call.
111111111.title: More Changes

1111111111.content:
My mouth was really hurting when Samara came back with a bag. The first thing she took out was a bottle. "This is Be'qun," she said. "It'll help you with the pain." She rubbed it on my gums, and my mouth felt better.

After a few minutes, she reached into the bag again, and pulled out a chocolate bar. I remembered the doctor saying that chocolate could make me feel happier. "This should help with your mood swings," she said.

"I could use it," I replied, and took a piece.

They were right: the chocolate made me feel much better. "What are my teeth going to be like when this is over?" I asked.

"Like mine," she said, and showed me her own teeth.

"They look like human teeth."

"But they're much stronger, and much sharper. We can eat nuts shell and all, as humans eat apples."

"Doesn't that lead to injuries? What happens when you bite someone?"

"Bite someone? Who would want to do that? Believe me, once you've cut your tongue on your teeth, you won't even consider such a thing."

"I'm sorry. I guess we humans don't look like such good neighbors, if biting isn't something we've put behind us."

"We're in no position to be fussy."

I opened my mouth to say something, and something fell on my tongue. Using my tongue, I brought it to my lips, and took it out.

It was a tooth. A molar, I think. "My teeth are coming out."

"Your Metaturian teeth will replace them. And then, I can bring you some nuts."

"I'll have to try eating them, shell and all."

"I'll let you in on a secret only we Metaturians know: The shell is the best part."

Then she reached into the bag again. "I almost forgot, you'll need to practice with these." She gave me a box of plastic forks.

"What's so hard?"

Samara replied by sticking a fork into her mouth and biting it. When she pulled her hand away, the business end of the fork was gone. She then pulled that out of her mouth.

"Can I use metal flatware?"

"Yes. But you'll have to deal with plastic forks at parties, so you'll need to learn not to do that."
1111111111.title: Teething

11111111111.content:
"I think now is as good a time as any for The Talk." Serious, Mom said. "I have a feeling it's a bit different than for humans."

Samara nodded. "Indeed it is." Calmly, she replied. She turned to me. "You see, Rennie, Metaturians do not use small talk in courtship rituals, as it is seen as selling oneself, like what you would call a prostitute."

"What do they, err, we use then?" Wincing, I asked.

"Hand signals and body language." simply, Samara replied. "Be fair warned, the closer a Metaturian male comes to you, the more interested he is."

"That's creepy." Shuddering, I said.

Samara smiled. "Many a Metaturian male learned the hard way, while trying to court a human female, to keep a polite distance." Bemused, she said. "Personal space is very important to your species, especially when it comes to courting."

Mom giggled at that. "Human males learn that the hard way, also."

She and Samara laughed at that, then turned back to me. "Metaturian courtship begins with eye contact, what humans see as 'staring'." Serious, Samara explained. "Once eye contact is made, they move their eyebrows up and down to express interest."

"Let me guess... " softly, Mom said. "A smile or frown indicates a return of or rejection of interest."

"You are close." Bemused, Samara said. "A smile or frown are indicators, but it is the rise of the eyebrows that shows a return of interest. Humans raise their eyebrows to show surprise, which was the beginning of many misunderstandings."

"What does a smile or frown indicate?" curious, I asked.

"A smile states the female is single, while a frown states she has interest in another." Serious, Samara replied. "Still, the raising of the eyebrows loudly states an interest."

"Where does it go from there?" Wincing, Mom asked.

"From there, it is up to the male to take the risk of coming closer." Calmly, Samara said. "You see, he may not be the only one catching her eye in a crowd."

"Is... touching a part of the courtship?" Nervous, I asked.

"At your age, no." Serious, Samara replied. "That is considered Ta-boa, or intent of passion assault. If proven to be the case, the male in question is... what is the term... sterilized for six months, as a warning."

"I take it the second time is worse." Gulping, I said.

Samara nodded. "Yes, but you are really too young to be worrying about that, dear." Calmly, she said. "If the female gives all indications that she is interested, only hand touching is permitted at first." She smiled. "Again, the risk is all on the male to come that close to a female."

"Why is it the boy's risk?" Taken aback, Mom asked. she gasped. "Don't tell me the males get pregnant!"

Samara laughed, shaking her head. "No! No! Males do not get pregnant, though I'm sure many females would love to see it happen." Amused, she said.

"So... why are the boys taking a risk?" Curious, I asked.

Samara giggled before showing her hand. Her fingernails seemed to grow instantly to a full inch long, then shrink back down to normal length. "Female Metaturians have retractable fingernails, much like a cat's claws." Bemused, she said. "They are not enough to cause a lot of damage, but, I can assure you, they hurt, and send a clear signal to the male that his advances are not welcome."

I sighed, trying to take it all in. "Is there anything else I need to know?"

Mom and Samara giggled. "We're just getting started, young lady." Bemused, Mom replied. "Make yourself comfortable... "
11111111111.title: The Talk

111111111111.content:
"I suppose we should prepare you for puberty, Rennie," Mom said. Turning to Samara, she said, "I guess it's pretty much the same for Metaturians as it is for humans? Breasts, acne, periods?"

"Yes, that sounds familiar," Samara replied. "One more breast than you, obviously. Acne, I assume, is infection of the pores? Yes, we have that, too. We mentioned periods yesterday."

"What's PMS like for you?" Mom asked. "I know I'm a mess before Aunt Flo - "

"I'm sorry," Samara said, "but what is PMS?"

"PMS. You know: cramps, bloating, irritability."

Samara shook her head. "We don't have that. Just an elevated temperature."

"Okay, now you've made me jealous of Rennie. Next, you'll tell me pregnancy for you is just nine months of sunshine and rainbows."

"Well, my pregnancy nearly killed me, so take that as you will. As for the duration, I'm afraid it's 11 months."

Mom fell silent, either from the shame of bringing up Samara's difficulties, or imagining herself having to carry this baby for three more months instead of one.

Samara turned to me. "As you would if you had remained human, you will need to bathe at least once a day, or you will start to smell. The difference is, while humans have a rather musky scent when they don't bathe, we smell more like an Earth coconut. I gather they like our body odor, but it's unpleasant to us. Differences." She shook my hand. "When you're older, I'll tell you about the big things: things two Metaturians like to do together, you know."
111111111111.title: The Talk continues.

1111111111111.content:
Mom and Samara's Talk left me with a lot to think about. I went to bed that night, after losing three more of my teeth, and woke up finding two more lying among my covers. Fortunately, there was no blood, or any real pain, thanks to the Be'qun and light chocolate. Looking at my fallen teeth, I sarcastically thought, The tooth fairy is going to make me rich.

At breakfast, Mom made me soft foods, since I was losing my human teeth, and my Metaturian teeth hadn't come in yet. I discovered that I could almost name all the ingredients of my food, as if my taste buds were becoming more sensitive. For the first time, I was not surprised, as Samara was able to name everything in Mom's homemade potato salad. It was just another thing I would have to get used to. I sighed, catching Mom's attention. She came and sat at my side by the table. "Are you alright, sweetie?" Concerned, she asked.

"It's just a lot to take in." Forcing a smile, I replied. "I'll be okay."

Mom smiled and lightly kissed me on the forehead, making me blush, though I wasn't sure why. "One step at a time, Rennie." Calmly, she said. "We'll help you through this."

While I was helping Mom clear the table minutes later, a polite knock came to the door. Curious, I answered, to find Corran standing there with a small handful of flowers. Before I could think, I raised my eyebrows in surprise. Corran smiled and began to move his eyebrows up and down, offering the flowers. If I wasn't blushing before, I certainly was then.

Fortunately, before the situation became too awkward, a familiar figure stepped up behind Corran. It was his father, Khoc, who chuckled at my lost expression. "My son has ke'lan-gor, or puppy-love." Bemused, he explained. "He told me that he feels you need a strong Metaturian male to look after you."

Now I was really blushing. Mom quietly giggling behind me was not helping. Slowly, timidly, I accepted the flowers and sniffed them. Corran, seeing this, turned to his father, uncertain, who gave a kind smile. "Hes'a ke'lan-gor." Calmly, he told him. "She returns your shy crush."

Corran and I both blushed at that point, staring at each other awkwardly, until Mom cleared her throat. "Are you going to let our guests in, Rennie?" Bemused, she asked.

I jumped at that and stepped aside, blushing more than before. Both Mom and Khoc were chuckling at their children's first crush.
1111111111111.title: Ke'lan-gor - A shy crush.

11111111111111.content:
"I'm sorry I got you into this," Corran said. "I had no idea the teleporter would do this to you."

"Nobody did," I replied. "But I'm getting through it, thanks to your mom."

"How's it going so far?"

"I'm losing my human teeth. I've got a smile like a Jack-o-lantern, now." I showed him.

"That won't last too long," he promised. "By this time next week, you'll have all your teeth again."

"I hope so. I could really use some chocolate."

As if on cue, Mom gave me a glass of chocolate milk. I thanked her even before I took a sip.

"You'll be alright, Rennie. This may be an aayala for you. For the world, even."

"Aayala?"

"I think the closest in English is 'blessing in disguise.' You'll be a beautiful Metaturian."

I was touched by what Corran said, and I gave him a kiss on the cheek. A moment spoiled only by my having to spit out Tooth No. 7 right after. "Another one for the box," I said.

"Box?"

"What else do you do when your teeth fall out? You put them in a box. Don't worry, I won't be hanging on to my hair the same way. Just something to remember the old me by."

"There is no old you, Rennie. There is no new you. There is only you. And you're easy to remember." Now it was his turn to kiss me on the cheek.
11111111111111.title: Talking with Corran.

choices: Sarah's mother comes for her daughter.
111111111111111.content:
While Corran and I continued to talk, my human friends came over for a visit. They all giggled at the fact I had a boy in my bedroom, making us both blush. "I'm jealous." Grinning, Sarah said. "My parents won't let any boy anywhere near my bedroom."

"I really didn't think about it." Honestly, I replied. "We were just talking."

"About... ?" Also grinning, Missi asked.

"Puberty." Chiming in, Corran replied. "It's different for Metaturians."

"For example, I'm losing all of my teeth, in exchange for harder, sharper ones." Giving a Jack-o-Lantern smile, I said. "Also, chocolate is a mood modifier for Metaturians."

"It does the same for humans." To my surprise, Susan said. She grinned, adding, "I have some when I'm feeling down, and it helps."

"I think we interrupted something." Giggling, Susan said.

"Human girls love to gossip." Blushing, I explained.

Corran sighed. "Wy'ti." Calmly, he said. "Hearsay conversations... Metaturian girls do the same thing."

We all giggled at that before tooth number eight fell out. "Another one for the box." Sighing, I said. "At this rate, I'll have my new teeth by the weekend."

"Anything else different with Metaturian puberty?" Curious, Sarah asked.

I explained all that I was told regarding puberty, before wincing, shutting my eyes with a painful moan.

"What's wrong?" Worried, Missi asked.

"Her eyes are changing." Wincing, Corran replied.

"Another part of puberty?" Wincing, Sarah asked.

Corran shook his head. "No, this usually happens when Metaturians are still babies, but Rennie is turning into one of us."

When I was finally able to open my eyes, everyone gasped, seeing that my irises now had threads of iridescence in them. "Everything looks strange." Wincing, I said.

"We see things differently than humans." Simply, Corran said. "Mom told me that it's like a human looking through a glass of water."

"This is so weird... "

"I want to try that." Getting up, Missi said.

She left then came back with Mom and a clear glass of water. "Oh wow... " Looking through the glass, she said. "This is so freaky!"

The girls took turns looking through the glass to experience how I was seeing them, while Mom called Samara while comforting me. To be honest, I wasn't afraid: I had my family and friends with me.
111111111111111.title: Friends Visit Me

1111111111111111.content:
Samara came right over, and came right to me. "May I have a look at your eyes, dear?" she asked.

I let her look. "Corran says it'll be like looking through a glass of water," I said.

She sighed. "That's not quite what I said, son," she said. "I said that humans see things as if we were looking through a glass of water."

"Really?"

"It will be as if you were seeing the world for the first time, Rennie. You will see farther than ever before, and you will see details you never even imagined." She gave me a pair of dark glasses. "Your eyes will need rest as they change. Take short naps when you need to."

"I'll be sure to," I said, then inadvertently spit out two of my teeth. "I guess it won't be long before my new teeth start coming in."

"That may be when the pain is worst, Rennie, so be warned."

I sighed. "Grellan-gex, my eyes, my teeth,... Does this ever stop hurting?"

Samara hugged me. "It will be better, precious child. This I promise you."

"We're all behind you, Rennie," Mom said. "We'll help you through."

I hugged them both.

When I let go, I wanted to hug them again. Because I saw the back of my hand, and a speck of gold there.
1111111111111111.title: Learning to see again.

choices: All my human teeth were out.
11111111111111111.content:
While we were talking, Sarah's mom arrived, and pulled her daughter away from us with a look of concern on her face. "Honey, what did I tell you?" Whispering harshly, she scolded. "Don't get too close to... them."

"They're my friends, Mom." Wincing, Sarah said.

Mrs Douglas sighed. "Look at Rennie... do you want that to happen to you?"

"That was an accident, Mrs. Douglas." Defending himself, Corran said. "No one could have known... "

"That's just it, young... alien." Firmly, Mrs Douglas said. "There are too many unknowns, far too many for me to be comfortable allowing my daughter anywhere near your kind."

"Our kind...?" Hurt, Corran asked.

"I sense a repeat of history." Startling Mrs Douglas, Khoc said. "Hatred and distrust seem to be deeply rooted in, how did you put it... your kind."

Mrs Douglas obviously did not like her own words thrown back at her. "I have the right to protect my daughter." Softly, she said.

"We reserve the same right." Calmly, Khoc countered. "Cor'et ta'l o'in wy'ti... Listen to the facts, not hearsay conversations."

Mrs. Douglas huffed, then led Sarah out of the house without another word. After she was gone, Corran turned to his father tearfully. "Does she hate Metaturians...?"

"She has Rue'tat Wor'tal." Sighing, Khoc replied.

Seeing my confused expression, Corran translated. "The fear of what one does not understand."

"The fear of the unknown." Calmly, Khoc corrected. "Sadly, what humans fear, they usually attack in some form."

That night, I went to bed feeling a bit frightened.

When I woke up,...
11111111111111111.title: Sarah's Mom

111111111111111111.content:
That morning, I woke up feeling chilled. Being half awake, I didn't take much notice, and decided to take a hot shower to warm up. I started the water and, once it was to a likable temperature, stepped in to a surprise that made me fully awake as I tried to wash my hair.

I had no hair to wash, I was completely bald! What the hell!? Freaked, I thought.

I jumped out of the shower and looked at the mirror in horror. I had no hair anywhere on my body, I looked just like a store mannequin, fresh out of the factory. Before I could rationally react, a shrill scream echoed from my throat.

Mere seconds later, an urgent knock came to my door and I heard Mom's voice on the other side. "Rennie, are you alright in there!?"

Wrapping a towel around myself, I opened the door and let Mom in before closing it again. "I'm bald, Mom!" Distraught, I wailed to her. "I have no hair!"

Mom sighed. "Well, you knew this was coming, young lady." Ruefully, she said. "I guess the spots will develop later on."

"Until then, I might as well be a mannequin at the local mall." Pouting, I said.

"They are hiring." Teasing, Mom replied. She then turned serious. "This is just a natural part of your development, alien puberty."

"Alien puberty." muttering, I said. "Terrific."

Mom giggled. "It's not so bad." Hugging me, she said. "Take your shower, then come down for breakfast. Having no hair is not the end of the world."

I came down to breakfast a few minutes later, to find the rest of the family at the table. Karen was looking into her phone, Dad was talking on the telephone while sipping on a cup of coffee, and Jeff was happily munching on his cold cereal. And then I showed up.

Dad looked up and sighed, having an idea of what I was going through, while Jeff dropped his spoon in shock, alerting Karen.

"Rennie! What happened?" Stunned, Karen asked. "You're bald!"

"Thank you, 'Captain Obvious'." Sarcastically, I replied. "I'm going through alien puberty, remember."

Jeff looked at me, lost, and signed, *Huh?*

Mom giggled. *Metaturians don't have any hair, dear.* Kindly, she explained. *It's just a part of who she is now.*

*Is that going to happen to me?* Worried, Jeff signed.

Mom shook her head. *No, dear.* Giggling, she signed, *I'll tell you more about it when you're older.*

*Always when I'm older.* Pouting, Jeff signed.

Dad smiled while Mom again giggled. *You will survive.*
111111111111111111.title: I had lost my hair.

1111111111111111111.content:
While I was busy eating breakfast, brooding over having no hair, Mom called Samara, who came over minutes later. She smiled upon seeing me. "Yun Entath." Softly, she said. "Happy rebirth."

I tilted my head to the side, confused. "Happy... rebirth?"

Samara nodded. "Normally, this is said to those who have recovered from a near fatal injury, but it suits this as well."

"Because I'm becoming Metaturian." Sighing, I said.

"It's not so bad." Smiling, Samara said. She then turned serious. "You will need to avoid direct sunlight for a few days, until your protective spots develop."

"Is that what the spots are for?" Curious, Mom asked. "Sunscreen?"

Samara giggled. "Among other things, yes." Calmly, she replied. "The protective spots help regulate our temperature, as well as help us fight off infections." She then turned back to me. "You make a very attractive Metaturian girl, Rennie."

"Um... thank you." Blushing, I replied. I then winced. "Ow!"

Mom jumped, startled, before she saw me spit out what was left of my teeth. I began to cry, as my mouth really began to hurt. Mom hurried to my side as my shaking hands rose to my mouth. Samara jumped in and put what looked like a large pacifier into my open mouth. "Just chew on it." Firmly but kindly, she said. "The discomfort will ease up in a minute."

"What's wrong with her?" Worried, Mom asked.

"It is called Rogat... " Calmly, Samara explained, "Teething. As Rennie is basically being reborn, I made it a habit of having a bag handy with everything you may need."

Mom nodded. "I appreciate that."

Chewing on the pacifier, although a bit embarrassing, did help ease the pain after a few moments. I tried to talk, but the pacifier muffled my words. I tried to take it out, but Samara stopped me with a gentle hand. "Save your questions for now." Kindly, she said. "Just keep chewing on that until it's all gone."

"It's edible?" Surprised, Mom asked.

"It's a mouth-numbing medication for infants." Simply, Samara explained. "Once the pacifier part completely dissolves, the rest becomes a toy for the infant." She giggled. "It's obvious that Rennie is not an infant, but it will still help her."
1111111111111111111.title: Mom calls Samara.

11111111111111111111.content:
For the rest of the day, I only took the teether out at mealtimes, or when I chewed one down to the toy. In addition to giving me medication, the teethers tasted good. After all, you wouldn't want a baby to spit it out instead of chewing on it, would you? Eventually, Mom gave me a tablet, so I could write down whatever I wanted to say.

By the time my friends came to see how I was doing, I had a baby toy on top of my dresser. "How you feeling, Rennie?" Martin asked. "What is that, a pacifier? And what's this on your dresser?"

"She's teething," Corran explained. "The toy is what's left when the teether is gone."

I nodded, and wrote, I don't care what it looks like. My mouth feels better.

"Well, that explains that," Susan said. "Wanna go to the park?"

I shook my head. Normally, I would. But I can't go out in the sun until I get my spots.

"That settles that," Martin said. "We'll just have to hang out here with you, until your spots come in."

And so we did.

We talked for a while about what was going on with my friends, so I wouldn't monopolize the conversation. Susan was getting along better with Chris McDonald, a boy who used to pull her hair. Finally, she explained to him that she didn't want to be with anyone who hurt her, and he started being nicer.

I took my dark glasses off to rub my eyes. "Whoa," Missi said.

I looked at her. Did she always look like that?

"I forgot your eyes were changing," she explained. "But they really did, didn't they?"

I switched the tablet to the camera function. Sure enough, my eyes were iridescent, with vertically slit pupils. They were Metaturian eyes.

"Are you okay, Rennie?" Iriel asked. "What's wrong?"

I used to have my father's eyes and my mother's hair, I explained. Now, I have neither.

"You'll be okay, Rennie," Galen said. "It's just part of the process."

I supposed he was right. I was becoming less like my family, but I was also becoming more like Corran.
11111111111111111111.title: Teething.

111111111111111111113.content:
The following morning, Mom and Samara took me back to see Dr. Olkaela at the local hospital, to check up on my transition progress. I was first met by a Metaturian nurse with golden skin and tan-like spots. She took my blood pressure and temperature, both with an odd bracelet, then pointed a strange light in my eyes that seemed to change color every few seconds.

"Your vitals are slightly elevated." Calmly, she said. "Nothing to raise any concern, but it should still be monitored until the transition is complete." She smiled. "Yun Entath."

"Thank you." Blushing slightly, I said.

Moments later, we were seen by Dr. Olkaela. "Based upon your progress thus far, I would say that your new teeth will be in within a few days, and your spots will fully develop within a week or two." Calmly, she said. "All in all, you are turning out to be a very attractive Metaturian girl."

"Um... thank you." Blushing again, I said.

Dr. Olkaela then sighed. "As for your elevated vitals, I would recommend you relax a little on the exercise until the transformation is complete."

"Is something wrong, doctor?" Worried, Mom asked.

"Your daughter is about to enter the most challenging hurdle of her transition." After a pause, Dr. Olkaela explained. "You see, physically, she is basically a toddler in the body of a ten year old, and is about to enter Wot Hodiz, or 'terrible twos'. "

"The terrible twos... " Sighing, Mom said. "Perfect... "

Mom giggled slightly after that, but Dr. Olkaela remained serious. "The terrible twos for Metaturians are quite different from that of humans." Firmly, she explained. "For humans, it is an emotional roller coaster. For Metaturians, it's physical in nature. Freezing one moment and frying the next, with their vitals jumping all over the board."

"What causes this?" Worried, Mom asked.

"It is because of the absence of spots, and the absence of her additional fingers and toes and her tail." Serious, Samara said. "Until they develop, you will have to carefully regulate the temperature around her."

"Rennie will be susceptible to colds and even pneumonia, as well as overheating, as the spots, tail, and sixth digits develop and begin to regulate her temperature."
111111111111111111113.title: Seeing Dr. Olkaela.

1111111111111111111131.content:
Two days later, I hit Wot Hodiz. It had me in tears as much as the human "terrible twos" would have. One minute, I was wrapped in our heaviest winter blanket, and I was still shivering. The next, I would be lying on top of my bedding, without a stitch on, and I was still sweating. Needless to say, I didn't leave my room.

During one of my naked and sweating phases, Samara came in, followed by Mom, and carrying a piece of equipment. "This should help you greatly, Rennie," Samara said.

"Is it a gun?" I asked.

"Oh, it's nothing you will regret my giving you, I assure you. No, this is a sudaqun, an environmental regulator. When you start shivering or sweating, this will adjust immediately, and alter the room temperature within moments, so you will be comfortable."

She put some kind of collar around my neck, then turned on the sudaqun. It started working almost immediately, and brought the temperature down to where I was comfortable in my nightgown again. "Thank you."

"You are more than welcome." With that, she left.

"Mind if I borrow your blanket?" Mom asked, then wrapped herself in it. "You're doing well, I'd say. You're turning a lovely shade of gold." She checked my mouth. "You'll be back on solid food in a day or two."

"I'm sorry, Mom."

"For what?"

"I don't look like you and Dad anymore."

Mom pulled me into a hug. "Oh, Rennie. I never loved you because you looked like me. I love you because you're my daughter, and nothing will ever change that."

I returned the hug. "It's just so hard," I said. "So incredibly hard."

"I think I understand a little of what you're going through. It sounds a bit like being pregnant. When it started, I was sick as a dog. Then my body changed in oh so many ways, and it was impossible to get comfortable. It was like being on a speeding train with no control. But then I had my children, and it was alright again. Your big sister was worth the train ride. You were worth the train ride. Your little brother was worth the train ride. And this one will be worth the train ride." She kissed me. "You're on a different train right now, but I promise you, it will be worth it. And then we'll celebrate with your favorite drink."

"Which one? Human or Metaturian?"

"You pick."

"Human."

"151 proof rum it is."
1111111111111111111131.title: The 'terrible twos'.

choices: My friends see the new me.
11111111111111111111311.content:
Wot Hodiz, the terrible twos, were indeed terrible. Fortunately, it only lasted two weeks rather than two months for normal Metaturian children. My new teeth came in through fits of heat and cold, making it uncomfortable, but the sudaqun and a little bit of chocolate helped keep it somewhat tolerable. By the end of two weeks, my skin was a light golden color with almost diamond colored spots. Looking in the mirror, I found that I had three budding breasts and six digits on each hand and foot.

"I look like an Emmy Award." Grinning slightly, I muttered.

I turned to Mom's giggle. "You look beautiful, Rennie." Kindly, she said. "Please get dressed, I just finished talking with Dr. Olkaela. She gave us the all clear to feed you solid foods again."

I smiled at that, eager to get away from mushy oatmeal and protein shakes for a while. Upon reaching the dining room, Jeff signed, *You look good.*

I smiled and signed back, *Thank you.*

Jeff looked at me for a moment, as if confused, then smiled and signed, *Your signing does have an accent.*

I giggled at that, then took my seat at the table next to Karen, who nudged my shoulder with a fist bump. "At least you won't be hard to find in the dark." Teasing, she said.

I grinned at her humor. "I'm still the queen of hide and seek."

Karen and I then started batting at each other's hands in a mock scuffle, giggling. Jeff chuckled at the sight, while Mom merely shook her head. "Enough, you two." Bemused, she said. "We have to go shopping for Metaturian clothing for Rennie today, and I, for one, would like to be done before lunch."

With that, breakfast was served. I enjoyed eating a plateful of sausage and eggs with a bagel and walnuts on the side. I took great care not to bite my tongue, as I found that my teeth were perfect for biting into truly solid foods. I bit right through the shells and chewed them into fine crumbs, easy to swallow.

"Samara was right." After swallowing, I said. "The shell is the best part."
11111111111111111111311.title: Solid Food Again

111111111111111111113112.content:
We managed to get my clothes in less than an hour and a half, which meant Mom could pick up lunch at the drive-thru. The smells were wonderful, except for the sodas, which reminded me of the day we found out what was happening to me. But I couldn't begrudge the others having something they could enjoy without harm, especially after Mom stopped at the liquor store for my rum.

When we got home, all my friends were there, waiting for me. So as not to disappoint, I spun around, showing not only my new outfit, but my completely Metaturian body.

"Looking good, Rennie," Missi said, coming closer. "If I'd known you would look this good, I'd have suggested we all go through the teleporter." She hugged me, then stepped back, feeling my torso. "A training bra? Already?"

"You know that Metaturian girls start puberty sooner than Earth girls," Corran explained.

"Don't get too jealous, Missi," I said. "Puberty also lasts longer for us, so I'll be having vrokaela the same time as you."

"Vro... Is that the curse?"

"Yes."

"It's not a curse," Iriel said. "It's simple biology."

"They know, Iriel. Humans call it 'the curse' because their hormonal changes are so upsetting to them."

"I can't get over how much you've changed," Sarah said. "Is this real?" She grabbed my tail.

I pulled away and turned. "Hands off! You don't see any of us grabbing your body parts, do you?"

"Sorry. I was just curious."

"Traditionally," Corran said, "only the mate touches a girl's tail, and only under... certain circumstances."

"Okay," Martin said, "nobody touches a tail unless we're invited."
111111111111111111113112.title: My friends see the new me.

1111111111111111111131121.content:
I had been a Metaturian for about a week.

Mom was still keeping an eye on us, but preferred to spend as much time as possible sitting down. We all knew that the baby would be born soon.

That morning, I sensed that something would happen. Every now and then, Mom would hiss under her breath and massage her lower back.

I mentioned it to Karen, and she nodded. "Her labor has probably started." Turning to Mom, she asked, "Do you need anything, Mom?"

"I'm fine, honey," Mom replied.

Karen turned back to me. "She's still in early labor. She'll tell us when it's more."

"Should we do something?"

"This is her fourth, Rennie. I think she knows how she's doing."

******************************


An hour later, Mom was reading, when she hissed again and clutched her belly. "Karen?" she asked.

"Mom?"

"Call your father. Tell him I need a ride. Now would be good."

That pretty much confirmed it: The fourth Weaver child was on its way.

As Karen called, Mom said, "Rennie, my bag is in the closet by my bed. Go and get it, and set it by the front door."

Dad must have set a land speed record getting home. He collected Mom and her bag, and left Karen in charge.

******************************


It was 9 o'clock at night when Dad called with the news: Mom had had a baby girl, who they named Veronica. "I'll call Samara," he said, "she'll drive you here, and you can all say hi to your baby sister."

In no time, we were at the hospital. Karen mentioned Mom's name, and a nurse nodded. "Right this way."

Before we went too far, another nurse stopped me. "I'm sorry," she said, "immediate family only."

"This is my sister," Karen said. "She changed, is all."

The first nurse whispered to the second, who blushed. "I'm so sorry," she said, and let me go with.

In the hospital room, we all hugged Mom, and Dad took pictures of each of us holding Veronica. Then he sent us home.

As Samara drove us home, though, I kept thinking about what the nurse said. She whispered low enough that others couldn't hear, but, to my Metaturian ears, she might as well have spoken plainly.

She was talking about me, and she said, "She's the transition survivor."
1111111111111111111131121.title: Mom Goes Into Labor

choices: I find I can read Metaturian writing.
I need to learn Metaturian writing and language.
11111111111111111111311211.content:
Upon returning home, I pulled Samara aside, with Mom following. "What did the nurse mean when she said I was the transition survivor?"

Samara sighed. "You heard that, did you?"

I nodded as Mom looked at us, confused. "Are you referring to the other people who didn't fare well during the transformation on other continents?"

Samara nodded. "Rennie is the only human to survive the full transition process that the hospital is aware of." Serious, she explained. "Rennie should consider herself fortunate that Dr. Olkaela and I have a lot of clout in Metaturian society."

I gulped hearing that. "I might have become a scientific curiosity... "

"Not so long as we can keep you out of the media spotlight." Serious, Samara replied. "So far, your case has been seen as an isolated incident, not really worth reporting." She then smiled. "As far as most are concerned, you are a Metaturian girl, because it is easier to accept than the truth."

"People more than not fear change." Softly, Mom said.

Samara nodded. "Enough of what could have been." Smiling, she said. "I'd like to offer you a few books on Metaturian history and culture, to give you a better idea of the world you have been reborn into."

I smiled slightly at that. "I'd like that."

Samara smiled back then left, returning a moment later with a number of books with strange writing on the cover.
11111111111111111111311211.title: Transition Survivor?

111111111111111111113112112.content:
Upon opening the first book, I knew that I was going to need some help. "This writing all looks a lot like ancient Egyptian hieroglyphics."

"Like what?" Curious, Samara asked.

Mom smiled, looking at the letters. "If they are, it's a dialect I don't recognize." Bemused, she said. "Did we just solve the mystery of the Great Pyramids?"

"Care to share what you are talking about?" Confused, Samara asked.

Mom walked into the other room, returning with a book on ancient Egypt, then opened it to a certain page. "The ancient Egyptians used a written language we call hieroglyphics." Calmly, she explained. "See... it's very similar to the Metaturian written language."

Upon comparing the two books, Samara giggled, much like a little girl in a candy store. "This is such a remarkable coincidence!" Excited, she said. "I thought we were the only species to use image lettering."

"Image lettering?" Curious, I asked.

Samara nodded. "Every species learns to talk before they learn to write." Simply, she explained. "We used images of legends and myths to build our alphabet."

"It's religious then?" Curious, Mom asked.

Samara laughed. "Not even close!" Amused, she replied. "Our ancestors simply used images that were easily recognized and had one to two syllable names." She pointed to the first symbol on the Metaturian book. "This is called Iye, the spirit of the skies... "

I wisely began to take notes as Samara explained the Metaturian alphabet. It took a while, but I was finally able to read the first line. "In the beginning, we were lost among the stars, with no world to call home... "

I teared up after reading that. "This book is how you found Earth?"

Samara nodded. "We found what you called the Voyager One probe, which had a number of images on it, giving us a vague but readable map to your planet."

I smiled at that. "So, in a sense, you were invited."

Samara nodded. "That's how we viewed it."

"That's so cool." Smiling, I said.
111111111111111111113112112.title: I need to learn Metaturian writing and language.

15.content:
Rennie/Aayala Weaver: Main character; changes from human to Metaturian

Human Characters

Richard Weaver: Main character's father
Scarlett Weaver: Main character's mother
Karen Weaver: Big sister
Jeff Weaver: Younger brother; hearing impaired
Veronica Weaver: Younger sister
Missi Pyle: Best friend
Susan Oliver: Best friend
Martin Stephens: Best friend
Sarah Douglas: Acquaintance; her mother is bigoted against Metaturians

Metaturian Characters

Samara Edozoik: Neighbor; guides Rennie through her metamorphosis; teaches her Metaturian culture
Corran Edozoik: Samara's son; develops feelings for Rennie
Galen Vulrn: Best friend
Iriel Straengi: Best friend
15.title: Cast of Characters

info.brief_description: Life isn't easy when you and your family are different species.
info.description:
This is the story of how Rennie Weaver becomes Aayala, a member of an alien race who have taken refuge on Earth.

Of course, you've gotta have rules:

1: Do not kill off the main character.
2: Try for correct spelling and punctuation.
3: Keep it PG-13. Nudity is okay. Strong language is okay, but don't overdo it. Sex is okay, but don't go into detail.
4: If you have an idea, run it by me, not somebody else who contributes to the story.
5: Check out my other interactives for an idea of how I work.

Ready? Let's go!
